title: [TRACK-C][PM-02] Generate evidence-tiered synthesis report and verdicts
description:
Generate the PM-02 synthesis/reporting layer.

For each hypothesis family, the report set must publish:
- opportunity count
- theoretical edge
- executable edge
- fill rate
- turnover
- capital path
- max drawdown
- ending bankroll distribution
- sensitivity table
- evidence tier
- final verdict state

The key repo question is: could a tiny bankroll plausibly reach extreme size under realistic assumptions?
acceptance_criteria: PM-02 reporting produces evidence-tiered verdicts per hypothesis family and a final synthesis that answers plausibility under realistic execution assumptions.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02][H2] YES+NO rebalancing reconstruction
description:
Test H2: repeated YES+NO < 1 single-market rebalancing as the driver of extreme compounding.

Question:
Could repeated single-market YES+NO rebalancing opportunities, captured at realistic depth and with realistic fees, explain the curve shape of a tiny bankroll growing to an extreme outcome?

Outputs must include executable edge, fill rate, turnover, capital path, max drawdown, ending bankroll distribution, and sensitivity table.
acceptance_criteria: H2 produces an evidence-tiered verdict on whether single-market YES+NO rebalancing could plausibly explain the claimed bankroll path.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02][H1] NegRisk compounding reconstruction
description:
Test H1: repeated NegRisk buy-all-YES opportunities as a compounding engine.

Question:
Could repeated NegRisk buy-all-YES opportunities, with realistic sizing, depth, fees, and missed fills, compound a tiny bankroll aggressively over time?

Outputs must include executable edge, fill rate, turnover, capital path, max drawdown, ending bankroll distribution, and sensitivity table.
acceptance_criteria: H1 produces an evidence-tiered verdict on whether NegRisk compounding alone could plausibly explain the claimed bankroll path.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02] Build path models for required edge, fill rate, turnover, and depth
description:
Build the PM-02 path models used to answer the reconstruction question.

The models should estimate what would have to be true, given a defined time window, to transform a tiny bankroll into an extreme endpoint.

Required outputs:
- minimum edge per trade
- required fill rate
- required turnover
- required market depth
- implied maximum drawdown
- opportunity half-life requirements
- sensitivity to fees and missed fills
acceptance_criteria: Path-model outputs let PM-02 quantify feasibility requirements instead of relying on a single lucky backtest curve.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02] Build fill simulator and compounder for tiny-bankroll path tests
description:
Build the PM-02 fill simulator and compounder for tiny-bankroll path testing.

Required features:
- bid/ask-based execution
- depth-aware fills
- partial fills
- entry and resolution fees
- stale-signal rejection
- venue/market concentration limits
- missed-fill accounting
- bankroll compounding logic
- market resolution timing
acceptance_criteria: Simulator rejects midpoint fantasy fills, models missed/partial fills, and produces bankroll paths under fee-adjusted and liquidity-constrained execution assumptions.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02] Build timestamped market-data recorder for reconstruction
description:
Build the market-data recorder needed for PM-02 reconstruction.

Recorder requirements:
- market metadata capture
- timestamped top-of-book snapshots
- full book snapshots when available
- trade / last-price capture when available
- venue + market partitioning in DuckDB/Parquet
- high-resolution timestamps suitable for replay and stale-signal analysis
acceptance_criteria: Recorder spec supports timestamped book/trade replay with venue-aware storage and enough detail to audit whether a claimed opportunity was actually fillable.

title: Build Claude combinatorial arb dependency detector
description: Use Claude to detect logically dependent market pairs for combinatorial arbitrage. Component B of prediction market arb system (epic: llm-quant-tced). Based on Saguillo et al. Section 5 methodology but upgraded from DeepSeek-R1 to Claude. The paper's prompt (Appendix B) asks for valid boolean truth-table combinations in JSON - we replace this with structured chain-of-thought reasoning. Algorithm: (1) Group markets by topic (Politics/Sports/Crypto/Economy/Technology) AND end_date (same day = likely same event). (2) For each market pair in same group, send to Claude: 'Given M1 {conditions} and M2 {conditions}: identify if any condition in M1 logically implies any condition in M2. If yes, identify dependent subsets S⊂M1, S'⊂M2. Does sum(val(S)) ≠ sum(val(S'))? By how much? Return JSON: {dependent: bool, S: [...], S_prime: [...], spread: float, confidence: float}'. (3) Only flag pairs with confidence >= 0.80. (4) Run checker: verify sum over dependent subsets satisfies Definition 4 from paper. Validation: reproduce the paper's 13 known dependent pairs from US election markets (see Appendix F pairs 1-13). If we find them, our detector works.
close_reason: Claude combinatorial dependency detector built at src/llm_quant/arb/detector.py. Structured chain-of-thought reasoning for logically dependent market pairs. Tests passing.

title: Build NegRisk arb detector (sum YES < 0.95)
description: Core arb detection logic for NegRisk (multi-condition) markets. Part of prediction market arb system (epic: llm-quant-tced). A NegRisk market has n mutually exclusive conditions (exactly one resolves True). Arb exists when: sum_i(val(YES_i, t)) < 1.0. Profit = 1 - sum_i(val(YES_i, t)). Implementation: (1) For each NegRisk market in DuckDB, fetch current YES prices for all conditions. (2) Compute sum. If < 0.95 (leaving buffer for 2% winner fee + slippage), flag as opportunity. (3) Log to pm_opportunities table: market_id, conditions, prices, spread, timestamp. (4) Focus on SPORTS markets first - paper explicitly notes these are underexplored. Kelly sizing: f* = spread/((spread + max_partial_loss_fraction) * bankroll). Cap at 2% bankroll per trade. Minimum spread threshold: 5 cents. Only scan markets where no single condition > 95% probability.

title: [PM] Boundary policy — structural edge vs directional forecasting
description:
Write the boundary policy separating structural/execution edge from directional/informational forecasting.

Structural / execution edge asks:
- was there a pricing inconsistency?
- could it be filled?
- what was net edge after fees/slippage?

Directional / informational forecasting asks:
- did we have an information advantage?
- was the market underreacting?
- how stable is that edge over time?
- are we overfitting narratives?

This policy should prevent future repo contamination between PM-01 / PM-02 structural work and PM-03 OSINT Event Alpha.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable boundary policy exists so future PM forecasting work cannot be mixed into Track C structural-arb evaluation without an explicit lane change and separate gates.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02][H4] Latency / microstructure plausibility test

Test H4: latency-sensitive microstructure as the explanation for extreme compounding.

Question:
Could short-lived orderbook lags or venue microstructure quirks explain the viral story, or would reproducing the path require lower-latency infrastructure than the repo currently assumes?

This is the hypothesis closest to the viral narrative and the hardest to validate honestly.

title: [TRACK-C][PM-02][H3] Combinatorial arb reconstruction

Test H3: combinatorial arbitrage bursts across related questions.

Question:
Could logical inconsistency across related questions produce high-ROE bursts that meaningfully contribute to extreme compounding, especially in clustered sports, election, or crypto event sets?

This work must stay deterministic at the decision boundary: LLMs may propose hypotheses, but validation and execution scoring must be rule-based.
acceptance_criteria: H3 produces an evidence-tiered verdict on whether combinatorial arbitrage contributes meaningfully under realistic validation, liquidity, and execution assumptions.

title: VIX contango-as-equity-timing REJECTED — redesign as VXX short
description:
VIX term structure contango (Family 4, attempt 1) REJECTED at backtest.
Slug: vix-term-structure-contango
Result: Sharpe=0.473, MaxDD=22.06%, DSR=0.850
Gates FAILED: Sharpe (0.473 < 0.80), MaxDD (22% > 15%), DSR (0.850 < 0.95)

ROOT CAUSE: Contango-as-equity-timing is a lagging signal. Contango is the normal
term structure (>80% of days), so the strategy is almost always long SPY. It exits
at market bottoms (when VIX spikes into backwardation — the worst time to sell) and
re-enters after recovery. This is the opposite of a useful signal.

MECHANISM INSIGHT: The correct way to harvest the VRP via term structure is to
SHORT volatility when contango is confirmed — buy SVXY or sell VXX, not buy SPY.
VXX bleeds continuously in contango (roll cost). This is the documented VRP trade
(Carr & Wu 2009, Simon & Campasano 2014) — not equity timing.

V2 HYPOTHESIS: When VIX3M/VIX ratio >= 1.05 (contango confirmed), go long SVXY
(short vol ETF) at 30% weight. Cash when ratio < 1.05. MaxDD gate is 15% for Track A
or 30% for Track B. SVXY at 30% is moderate vol exposure.

ALTERNATIVELY: Use the VX1-VX2 futures spread (bead bsgb) for a purer VRP signal.
VIX9D is available via Yahoo (^VIX9D) as a shorter-term vol proxy.

NOTE: Infrastructure change needed — need to add SVXY to universe.toml and verify
yfinance data availability. Check SVXY inception (February 2012).

title: [FAMILY-4-V2] VIX term structure via VXX short (vol-selling mechanism)
description: Family 4 second attempt: use VIX term structure signal to SHORT VOLATILITY (not time equity). Signal: VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (contango confirmed) → short VXX (equivalent to long SVXY). Exit: ratio < 1.00 (backwardation). v1 (equity long/cash) failed because contango-as-equity-timing is lagging. The VRP mechanism is real but requires capturing roll yield by being short front-month VIX futures via VXX. Track B gates: Sharpe>1.0 MaxDD<30% DSR>=0.95. Need to add VXX/SVXY to universe. Mechanism: VXX loses ~7% annually from contango roll in normal markets; short VXX harvests this roll yield + captures backwardation reversal. References: Simon & Campasano 2014, Whaley 2013 VRP literature.

title: Build Kalshi execution engine (US-legal)
description: Execute NegRisk arb trades on Kalshi (CFTC-regulated, US-legal). Component C of prediction market arb system (epic: llm-quant-tced). Kalshi REST API at https://trading.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2. Requirements: (1) Map detected arb opportunities to equivalent Kalshi markets (not all Polymarket markets exist on Kalshi). (2) Non-atomic execution risk: place limit orders on all YES legs simultaneously. Set timeout T=950 blocks equivalent (~1 hour). If any leg unfilled after T, cancel remaining orders. (3) Kelly position sizing: f* ~ 2% of bankroll at 5% spread, 90% fill rate. Formula: f* = (p_full * spread) / (spread + max_loss_fraction). (4) Track fills: DuckDB pm_executions table with order_id, leg, fill_price, fill_time, status. (5) Gate: only execute if fill_rate >= 0.85 in paper trading phase. Kalshi fee: 3% taker - minimum spread threshold becomes 5% (3% fee + 2% buffer). Replaces Polymarket execution (US restriction). Start with paper trading mode only.

Strategy: run bb_window=60/90/120 as a CONSENSUS portfolio -- enter only when 2 of 3 lookbacks agree on signal, exit when 2 of 3 agree on exit. This neutralizes the single-lookback sensitivity. Expected rho to credit family ~0.05 (calendar-driven, orthogonal to credit signals). Need OPEX date list (3rd Friday of each month).

title: [FAMILY-6] Yield curve momentum — full lifecycle

title: [P2] Marginal portfolio SR contribution gate greater than 0.05
description: Before admitting a new strategy to the portfolio, compute its marginal Sharpe contribution using: ΔSR_P ≈ (SR_k - ρ_{kP} × SR_P) / sqrt(1 + 2×ρ_{kP}×SR_k/SR_P). If ΔSR_P <= 0.05, skip — the strategy is too correlated or too weak to move portfolio-level Sharpe. Implementation: wire into /promote checklist. Requires knowing: (1) strategy individual SR, (2) correlation between strategy daily returns and portfolio daily returns, (3) current portfolio SR. Already documented in alpha-hunting-framework.md (Question 7) and extreme-sharpe-playbook.md kill list.

title: [P2] Portfolio correlation gate less than 0.30 for new strategy admission
description: Before admitting a new strategy to the portfolio, compute its correlation to the existing equal-weight portfolio returns. If rho >= 0.30, skip as redundant — the new strategy won't meaningfully improve portfolio SR. Formula from extreme-sharpe-playbook.md: ΔSR_P ≈ (SR_k - ρ_{kP} × SR_P) / sqrt(1 + 2×ρ_{kP}×SR_k/SR_P). Implementation: wire this gate into the /promote checklist (promote.md) as a hard check. Already documented in alpha-hunting-framework.md (Question 6) and extreme-sharpe-playbook.md kill list.

title: Add volatility targeting to BacktestEngine
description: Add vol-targeting layer to BacktestEngine: scale each strategy's position size by (sigma_target / sigma_hat_t) where sigma_hat is trailing 20-day realized vol and sigma_target = 0.10 (10% annualized). Cap leverage at 1.5x. Test: does vol-targeted portfolio improve portfolio Sharpe vs fixed weight? Moreira and Muir (2017): this is a free lunch. See docs/research/implementation-gaps.md. Add vol_targeting: {enabled, sigma_target, max_leverage} to research spec.

title: Add HRP portfolio construction script
description: Implement Hierarchical Risk Parity weights for the 11-strategy portfolio. Steps: (1) compute pairwise correlation matrix (scripts/compute_correlation_matrix.py already exists), (2) apply hierarchical clustering with correlation distance d=sqrt(0.5*(1-rho)), (3) quasi-diagonalize, (4) recursive bisection with inverse-variance weights. Use PyPortfolioOpt or implement from scratch. Output: optimal HRP weights vs current equal weights. Show how credit-equity cluster gets downweighted. Add as scripts/compute_hrp_weights.py.

title: Add MinTRL computation to robustness output
description: Compute Minimum Track Record Length for each backtest: MinTRL = (1 - skew*SR + ((kurt-1)/4)*SR^2) * (z_{0.95} / (SR - SR*))^2 where SR*=0. Flag strategies where backtest_length < MinTRL. Add to src/llm_quant/backtest/robustness.py and report in robustness.yaml. See docs/research/institutional-quant-guide.md for formula. For SR=0.5 with normal returns at 95% confidence: ~60+ months required.

title: [M3-v2] SPY-TLT correlation hysteresis band (entry -0.05, exit +0.05)
description:
**Mandate M: Correlation / Regime Signals**

**Hypothesis:** SPY-TLT 20d rolling correlation with hysteresis band (go defensive when corr < -0.05, resume SPY when corr > +0.05) generates Sharpe > 0.7 while keeping MaxDD < 12%.

**Mechanism:** Filters noise near the zero-correlation boundary. Entry at -0.05 ensures genuine regime shift (not measurement noise). Exit at +0.05 prevents premature re-entry. Economically: the 2022 inflation shock demonstrated that SPY-TLT correlation can break positive for extended periods; the hysteresis band provides a more stable signal than simple sign-change.

**Motivation:** M3 perturbation analysis showed exit_thresh=-0.05 gives Sharpe=0.956 MaxDD=9.55% vs base Sharpe=0.555 MaxDD=14.9%. This parameter change MUST be pre-specified in the research-spec before backtesting.

**Universe:** SPY, TLT
**Measurement:** Compute 20d rolling SPY-TLT correlation; hold SPY at 0.75 weight when corr > -0.05; exit to cash when corr < -0.05; resume entry only when corr > +0.05.
**Data:** Yahoo Finance (SPY, TLT)

**Lifecycle path:**
/mandate -> /hypothesis -> /data-contract -> /research-spec freeze -> /backtest -> /robustness -> /paper -> /promote

**Infrastructure:** existing: correlation_regime strategy class (add exit_threshold parameter support)
close_reason: REJECTED: M3-v2 hysteresis band FALSIFIED. Sharpe=0.243 MaxDD=9.27% DSR=0.689. Symmetric dead-zone kept strategy in cash during 2023-2025 bull run. Entry threshold -0.05 too conservative — strategy misses equity participation when corr hovers near zero after exit.

title: Add volatility scaling infrastructure for position sizing
description: Compute 126-day (6-month) realized variance for all universe assets. Implement inverse-variance position sizing that targets 12% annualized volatility per position. This is foundational infrastructure required by TSMOM (llm-quant-rbu) and future risk parity implementation. Barroso & Santa-Clara (2015) showed vol scaling nearly doubles Sharpe for equity momentum (0.53 to 0.97), reduces excess kurtosis from 18.24 to 2.68, improves skewness from -2.47 to -0.42. Cross-cutting finding from all 3 research briefs: vol scaling is the single most impactful technique. Files: src/llm_quant/data/indicators.py (realized variance computation), trading/executor.py (vol-adjusted sizing), risk/manager.py (integrate with existing risk checks). Should be built before or concurrently with TSMOM.

title: Implement multi-lookback blended TSMOM signals with vol scaling
description: Compute TSMOM signals at 3 lookbacks: 1-month (21 days), 3-month (63 days), 12-month (252 days). Equal-weight blend (low cross-correlation makes this near-optimal per Hurst et al. 2013, Baltas & Kosowski 2013). Apply volatility scaling using 126-day realized variance targeting 12% annualized vol per position (Barroso & Santa-Clara 2015). Supplements existing SMA crossover + RSI signals (Levine & Pedersen 2016 proved TSMOM and SMA crossovers are mathematically equivalent). Individual instrument Sharpe ~0.3-0.4 but portfolio Sharpe >1.0 through diversification. Note: Huang et al. 2020 challenge asset-by-asset predictability -- vol scaling may be primary return driver (Kim et al. 2016). Files: src/llm_quant/signals/tsmom.py (new), data/indicators.py (trailing returns at 21/63/252 days), brain/context.py, brain/models.py, config/settings.toml (lookbacks, vol target, blend weights).

title: Add credit spread (BAMLC0A0CM) as leading stress indicator
description: Fetch ICE BofA US Corporate Index OAS from FRED (series BAMLC0A0CM). Compute 20-day z-score. Surface in MarketContext as supplementary stress signal. Flag 'silent stress' when credit z-score > 1.5 AND VIX is still low (below 20). Do NOT use as standalone regime switch -- relationship is unstable (broke down 2 years post-COVID). Wellington 2022 documented credit leading equity vol during 2022 drawdown. Gilchrist & Zakrajsek 2012 showed EBP predicts recessions 12 months ahead. Files: src/llm_quant/data/fetcher.py (add FRED data source), brain/context.py (add _get_credit_spread()), brain/models.py (add credit_spread_zscore field to MarketContext), config/settings.toml (FRED API key, thresholds), config/templates/decision.md.j2. Low complexity, high value for 15% max drawdown constraint.

title: Implement 2-state HMM regime detection
description: Replace the current VIX + TLT/SHY + SPY trend heuristic in brain/context.py with a 2-state (risk_on/risk_off) Hidden Markov Model. Use weekly observations with ~130-day lookback window (Nystrup et al. 2019). Observation variables: VIX level, yield curve slope, SPY momentum. Gaussian emissions. Keep existing heuristic as fallback/comparison. The 'transition' state maps to low-confidence zone between HMM states. Literature: 2-state outperforms 4-state in portfolio performance (Fons et al. 2021). Files: src/llm_quant/regime/hmm.py (new), brain/context.py, brain/models.py (add regime_confidence to MarketContext), data/indicators.py (weekly aggregation), config/settings.toml ([regime] section). Depends on hmmlearn or custom impl. Expected impact: better state persistence, probabilistic confidence, disciplined sizing during transitions.

title: Flag USO as COT-ineligible and add data staleness fallback
description:

Part A - USO COT exclusion:

Part B - COT staleness detection:

title: Implement adaptive VIX regime classification
description: Replace fixed VIX thresholds (20/25) with historical percentile-based boundaries. A 2025 RegimeFolio study showed data-driven terciles (~17.8/23.1) achieved Sharpe of 1.17 vs 0.94 for fixed thresholds -- 24% improvement. Feed regime classification into LLM decision context and use regime to modulate risk parameters (position sizing, sector tilts). Three-regime structure (risk_on/transition/risk_off) is well-motivated but boundaries should adapt. Ref: research/results/3.1/output.md

title: Implement DCC-GARCH correlation estimation for risk checks
description: Replace rolling window correlations with DCC-GARCH (or EWMA lambda=0.94-0.97 as fallback). Engle (2002) proved rolling windows are the worst performer. Two-step estimation: 39 univariate GARCH models + 2 scalar DCC parameters, feasible in 30-60 seconds. Apply Forbes-Rigobon adjustment using volatility-standardized returns to avoid heteroskedasticity bias. Add diversification scoring to risk checks as a warning/advisory signal, NOT a hard trade blocker (the blocking mechanism is novel and untested). Requires arch Python package. Ref: research/results/3.1/output.md

title: Embed execution cost estimates in market context for friction-aware decisions
description: Research domain 1 established round-trip friction by asset class: US equity 2-6 bps, international 7-18 bps, EM 12-33 bps, treasury 3-9 bps, HY 8-24 bps, crypto ETF 3-9 bps, forex 17-18 bps (at 2500 USD). Add an [execution_costs] section to risk.toml with these estimates. Include them in MarketContext so the LLM can compare expected alpha against friction before entering trades. Particularly important for EM/forex where friction can exceed daily alpha. Files: config/risk.toml, src/llm_quant/brain/context.py, src/llm_quant/brain/models.py

title: Compute 126-day VIX percentile rank for conditional position sizing

Implementation: (1) fetch 126 days of VIX history in context.py, (2) compute percentile rank, (3) add vix_percentile field to MarketContext in models.py, (4) update decision prompt to reference VIX percentile for sizing guidance. 126-day lookback is optimal memory per Wysocki (2025). title: Build regime overlay meta-strategy for dynamic weight allocation
description: Phase 4 from alpha-hunting-framework.md. Build a meta-strategy that shifts portfolio weights based on VIX regime: (1) Risk-off (VIX>25): overweight trend-following, pairs, and defensive strategies; underweight credit lead-lag, (2) Risk-on (VIX<18): overweight credit lead-lag, underweight trend-following, (3) Transition: hold HRP weights. This is the COMBINE phase of the kill chain. Requires correlation dashboard and HRP weights as inputs. Expected improvement: +0.1-0.2 portfolio SR by adapting to regime shifts.

title: Research Family 5: Pre-FOMC drift strategy (calendar structural alpha)
description: Academic finding (Lucca & Moench 2015): ~80% of SPY equity premium accrues in the 24 hours before FOMC announcements. We tested F3 (month-end) and F4 (pre-FOMC TLT) -- both falsified by 2022 rate hike cycle. New hypothesis: pre-FOMC SPY LONG (not TLT) -- buy SPY at close 1 day before FOMC, exit at close on FOMC day. The drift is in equity, not bonds. Conditioned on: VIX < 25 (not in crisis) AND current rate cycle = cutting or neutral. Requires FOMC calendar data (available at federalreserve.gov or hardcoded). Family 5 calendar alpha is partially falsified but the equity drift may be more robust than the bond drift.

title: Test ML gate on all 14 passing strategies after single-strategy validation
description: Once SOXX-QQQ ML gate is validated, run run_ml_gate.py on all 14 passing strategies in the portfolio. Run as batch. For each: record base Sharpe, ML-gated Sharpe, block rate, preliminary gate results. Do NOT run full robustness on all 14 immediately -- only proceed to robustness for strategies where preliminary gates all pass. This is a screening step, not a promotion step. Expected: 3-5 strategies may show meaningful improvement; rest will be neutral or negative. Document all results.
close_reason: Upstream REJECTED: ML gate A/B on SOXX-QQQ showed zero improvement. Single-strategy validation failed -- no point testing all 14. ML gate adds no discriminative value for lead-lag strategies with 5-feature logistic regression.

title: Add multimodal chart pattern recognition signal
description:
Integrate vision-based chart pattern recognition as a new signal source in the Filters→Indicators→Signals→Rules framework. Inspired by QuantAgent (Y-Research-SBU).

Approach:
1. Render OHLCV data as candlestick chart images (mplfinance or similar)
2. Optionally overlay computationally-fitted trendlines (support/resistance)
3. Send chart PNG to a vision-capable LLM (Claude with vision)
4. Parse LLM response for classical patterns (H&S, double top/bottom, triangles, flags, wedges, etc.)
5. Output structured signal (pattern name, direction, confidence) that feeds into existing decision pipeline
6. Gate through existing risk checks and governance like any other signal

Key design decisions:
- This is a SIGNAL SOURCE, not a standalone trading system
- Must integrate with existing build_context.py / decision pipeline
- Pattern output must be structured (not free text) for systematic use
- Should support configurable timeframes (daily default, potentially 4h)
- No execution changes needed — just a new signal type

Reference: https://github.com/Y-Research-SBU/QuantAgent (arXiv:2509.09995)

title: Design inflation regime overlay (2x2 growth/inflation matrix)
description: Research spike + prototype: fetch TIPS breakevens from FRED (T5YIE for 5-year, T5YIFR for 5y5y forward). Classify inflation environment into 2x2 matrix: {rising/falling growth} x {rising/falling inflation}. Four quadrants: reflationary boom (overweight commodities/energy), disinflationary boom (overweight equities/credit), stagflation (overweight gold/TIPS), deflationary bust (overweight nominal bonds). BLOCKED on llm-quant-773 (risk regime must be stable first). Key challenge: stagflation detection is nearly impossible to predict onset (Cliffwater 2022), detection lag 2-4 months. TIPS breakevens accurate within 55-80 bps for >=2yr horizons but distorted by liquidity premium (~100 bps). Expected Sharpe improvement 0.07-0.20. Files: src/llm_quant/regime/inflation.py (new), data/fetcher.py (FRED sources), brain/context.py, brain/models.py (InflationRegime enum).

title: Implement Filtered Historical Simulation CVaR constraints
description: Add portfolio-level CVaR constraint using Filtered Historical Simulation (Barone-Adesi 1999), not raw historical sim. GARCH-filter residuals and rescale to current volatility. Extend lookback to 500+ days with EWMA decay weighting (lambda=0.94). Raw historical sim with 252 days produces only ~12-13 tail observations -- dangerously unstable (20-30% relative std dev). Include named stress scenario overlay (COVID, 2022 rates, crypto winter, SVB) as mandatory safety net to address cliff effect. Use proportional scaling enforcement (CPPI-style), not binary blocking. Compute bootstrap confidence intervals on CVaR estimates. Ref: research/results/3.3/output.md

DSR gate structural reality: DSR>=0.95 requires very high Sharpe ratio with small trial counts. Best DSR seen: D7=0.9390 (closest), M7=0.8833, K7=0.8645, A8=0.6435. DSR penalizes each additional trial in the family (harder with more trials). The 0.95 threshold may only be reachable with: (1) Sharpe>1.0 strategies, OR (2) very large sample T (many years), OR (3) reducing trial count by being more selective. Consider this before spawning large trial families.

corrected-portfolio-sr-formula: Combined SR with pairwise correlation rho (equal SR, equal weight): SR_P = SR_individual × sqrt(N / (1 + (N-1)×rho)). Our 11-strategy portfolio: avg rho=0.584, SR_P ≈ 1.35 (NOT 2.3). The 2.3 was wrong — it used N_effective approximation assuming zero correlation. Two new kill list gates added 2026-03-26: (1) portfolio correlation < 0.30 (bead llm-quant-r5j4), (2) marginal SR contribution > 0.05 (bead llm-quant-taff). Formula: ΔSR_P ≈ (SR_k - ρ_{kP} × SR_P) / sqrt(1 + 2×ρ_{kP}×SR_k/SR_P). Both in alpha-hunting-framework.md Q6/Q7 and extreme-sharpe-playbook.md kill list.
